TEST.0.title: 2026 Division C Regional Test - March Extra

CIPHER.491.cipherString: My teachers helped guide and motivate me; but the responsibility of learning was left with me.
CIPHER.491.question: The janitor found this strange paper strip under a Bunsen burner. It's a quote by Joseph Stiglitz, encoded with an Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.491.author: Joseph Stiglitz

CIPHER.474.cipherString: A doctor can bury his mistakes but an architect can only advise his clients to plant vines.
CIPHER.474.question: A riddle is discovered taped under a chemistry lab bench. It's a quote by Frank Lloyd Wright, encrypted as a Complete Columnar. The quote has STAKES somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.474.author: Frank Lloyd Wright

CIPHER.466.cipherString: The Eskimo have fifty-two names for snow because it is important to them; there ought to be as many for love.
CIPHER.466.question: Found frozen inside an ice core sample from the Arctic: this Aristocrat message by Margaret Atwood. You are told that the cipher starts with THE. What does it say?
CIPHER.466.author: Margaret Atwood

CIPHER.477.cipherString: Absence of occupation is not rest. A mind quite vacant is a mind distressed.
CIPHER.477.question: A weathered note is found inside an old science textbook. The writing appears to be a quote by COWPER encoded as a Checkerboard with a polybius key of OVERPLAYING. What does it say?
CIPHER.477.author: COWPER

CIPHER.475.cipherString: ¿Por qué no esperamos a que la vida en otros planetas nos encuentre? ¿Por qué tenemos que hacer todo el trabajo?
CIPHER.475.question: You receive this puzzling text during a live science broadcast. It contains a quote in Spanish, encrypted as a Xenocrypt using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.475.author: Unknown
CIPHER.475.translation: Why don't we wait for life on other planets to find us? Why do we have to do all the work?

CIPHER.472.cipherString: When I come up against the real world, I just vacillate.
CIPHER.472.question: Found on a microchip inside a disassembled robotics kit: this Baconian Cipher by Robert Crumb. What does it say?
CIPHER.472.author: Robert Crumb

CIPHER.481.cipherString: Sometimes you hold on to things, not because you want to keep them, but because they are difficult to let go.
CIPHER.481.question: You discover a scrap of paper tucked inside an old lab notebook. It contains a quote by Phaedra Patrick encoded as an Aristocrat. You are told that the cipher contains the word you twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.481.author: Phaedra Patrick

CIPHER.476.cipherString: Power does not concede without a demand, and neither does patriarchy,
CIPHER.476.question: A fragment of text is discovered in a time capsule from 1965. It's a quote by Monica Geingos encrypted as a Complete Columnar Cipher. The quote has DOESP somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.476.author: Monica Geingos

CIPHER.483.cipherString: It's like building a cathedral, in which only a few seconds of animation are completed each week.
CIPHER.483.question: A time-capsule USB drive from 1983 just auto-opened with this Aristocrat-encrypted message using a K2 alphabet. What was the K2 Keyword used to encode it?

CIPHER.468.question: Someone taped a piece of paper with this sequence onto the back of a trophy from last year's Science Olympiad. It's a quote, written using a Cryptarithm Cipher. What do the values 0344 67518 95812 decode to?

CIPHER.469.cipherString: Begin every New Year a year ahead. End every year three months ahead. Watch your goals be achieved.
CIPHER.469.question: During a field trip, you notice these strange markings on a metal plaque. They represent a quote by Richie Norton, encoded using an Aristocrat Cipher using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.469.author: Richie Norton

CIPHER.467.cipherString: Today is yours to shape. Create a masterpiece!
CIPHER.467.question: The school's ancient overhead projector flickered and burned this Baconian message onto the screen of a quote by Steve Maraboli. You are told that the deciphered text starts with TODA. What does it say?
CIPHER.467.author: Steve Maraboli

CIPHER.487.cipherString: Monkeys, who very sensibly refrain from speech lest they should be set to earn their livings.
CIPHER.487.question: Archaeologists unearth a stone tablet inscribed with a message. It's a quote by Kenneth Grahame encoded as an Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.487.author: Kenneth Grahame

CIPHER.490.cipherString: Self-denial is indulgence of a propensity to forgo.
CIPHER.490.question: Your science fair project logs contain a mysterious string of letters. It's a quote by Ambrose Bierce, encoded as a Fractionated Morse. You are told that the quote starts with SELFDE. What does it say?
CIPHER.490.author: Ambrose Bierce

CIPHER.485.cipherString: I had a vocal coach. It's a sad thing, but I had to hire someone so that I could get my Australian accent back.
CIPHER.485.question: Scrawled in dry-erase marker on the whiteboard after hours: this Aristocrat encoded quote by Anthony LaPaglia using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains I. What does it say?
CIPHER.485.author: Anthony LaPaglia

CIPHER.465.question: Found taped under a desk in study hall: this Hill puzzle by Bitsy Kemper encoded with a keyword of THUD. What does it say?
CIPHER.465.author: Bitsy Kemper

CIPHER.471.cipherString: Making decisions requires energy, but not deciding about whether to decide requires even more energy.
CIPHER.471.question: You find an encoded message in the test tube rack, written on masking tape. It's a quote by David Allen, written in an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains even. What does it say?
CIPHER.471.author: David Allen

CIPHER.478.cipherString: Do you want to tell the truth, or do you want to tell a story?
CIPHER.478.question: A vending machine just dispensed a candy wrapper with this Baconian code by Robert Charles Wilson. What does it say?
CIPHER.478.author: Robert Charles Wilson

CIPHER.479.cipherString: The preservation of liberty depends upon the intellectual and moral character of the people.
CIPHER.479.question: This coded note is discovered inside a sealed envelope labeled “Confidential.” It's a quote by David McCullough, encoded using an Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.479.author: David McCullough

CIPHER.486.cipherString: If you wish to subject everything to yourself, subject yourself first to reason.
CIPHER.486.question: A note falls out of a borrowed library book. The message is a quote by Seneca, written as a Checkerboard Cipher with a Polybius key of MARVELOUS. What does it say?
CIPHER.486.author: Seneca

CIPHER.484.cipherString: Problems can become opportunities when the right people come together
CIPHER.484.question: You receive this cryptic message from the future through an online time capsule. It's a quote by Robert South, written in a Complete Columnar. The quote has RIGHT somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.484.author: Robert South

CIPHER.480.question: This code is found written in chalk on the sidewalk outside the science building. It's a quote, written in a Cryptarithm Cipher. What do the values 86792 75 40132 decode to?

CIPHER.488.cipherString: All of science is nothing more than the refinement of everyday thinking.
CIPHER.488.question: A glitchy hologram in the school planetarium displays this Porta version of Albert Einstein's words encoded using a keyword of SWAYED. What does it say?
CIPHER.488.author: Albert Einstein

CIPHER.473.cipherString: The artist thought his favourite paint had been stolen, but it was just a pigment of his imagination.
CIPHER.473.question: A mysterious balloon release at the science fair carried this Aristocrat banner using a K2 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains the word his twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.473.author: Unknown

CIPHER.470.cipherString: I've put something aside for a rainy day. It's an umbrella.
CIPHER.470.question: A coded message appears after you shine a UV light on a classroom poster. It turns out to be a quote, encrypted using a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of BOTHER and polybius key of SHOULDERING. What does it say?
CIPHER.470.author: Unknown

CIPHER.489.cipherString: There is no abstract art. You must always start with something. Afterward you can remove all traces of reality.
CIPHER.489.question: You came across the following written on a wall in a cave. You are told that it's a quote by Pablo Picasso encoded as an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. What was the K1 Keyword used to encode it?
CIPHER.489.author: Pablo Picasso

CIPHER.482.cipherString: I'll never have enough time to paint all the pictures I'd like to.
CIPHER.482.question: An explorer's journal mentions a “hidden truth” encoded below. It turns out to be a quote by Norman Rockwell, written in a Nihilist Substitution Cipher with a keyword of PURELY and polybius key of CUSTOMARILY. What does it say?
CIPHER.482.author: Norman Rockwell
